threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know someone just moved in when they still think the bus will arrive on time. In Hackney, just assume it's a two-hour wait.
Caught the 196 last night, a guy started singing ‘Wonderwall’ at the back. He forgot the lyrics halfway, then just shouted ‘Live forever!’ instead.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was part of this place? Watching a guy sell homemade wine from his balcony while a street musician played a tune that's been stuck in my head ever since.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club party in a former public toilet—yes, that’s literally how we repurpose space here. Talk about creativity!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they navigate the BART delays—while newcomers stand confused, we’re already pulling out our phones to check the bus times.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Only in the Tenderloin does a Muni bus pause for a stray dog, causing a cascade of honks and missed transfers. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the squares and shops, but it’s the side streets with the slowly peeling facades where the real stories hide.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look puzzled at the 10pm dinner rush, clutching takeaway menus, while locals casually sip their vermut.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'tube' instead of the 'Overground'—big giveaway you're not yet part of the Dalston vibe.
Caught a bloke singing in French on the 333 last night, totally in his own world. Brixton's night bus vibe is like nowhere else.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a pop-up flea market on Sonnenallee, with everything from second-hand records to homemade hummus. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you order a currywurst at 3 AM from a street vendor while listening to a spontaneous live DJ set. It's a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they don’t even blink at a BART evacuation, just keep scrolling on their phone like it’s a Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The other day, a dog just chilled on the tracks at Civic Center. Muni was stuck for ages while everyone snapped pics. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a pa amb tomoquet anywhere, but the real gem is from that little bar on Carrer de Verdi, made by the old guy with the mustache.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll catch them checking the T10 ticket like it's some kind of mystical artifact, still unsure if they've punched it correctly before getting on the metro.
